channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: US officials have been in direct contact with the Syrian rebel group that ousted Assad, Blinken says
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken says American officials have been in direct contact with the Syrian rebel group that spearheaded the overthrow of President Bashar Assad’s government but is designated a foreign terrorist organization by the United States and others.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump brings political drama to Army-Navy game sidelines
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect JD Vance will attend the annual Army-Navy game in Landover, Maryland, on Saturday, and they are bringing some guests who are sure to stir debate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump hosts Apple CEO at Mar-a-Lago as big tech leaders continue outreach to president-elect
item.description: Donald Trump is hosting Apple CEO Tim Cook for a Friday evening dinner at the president-elect’s Mar-a-Lago resort.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Climate justice group has deep ties to judges, experts involved in litigation amid claims of impartiality
item.description: A controversial judicial advocacy organization funded by left-wing nonprofits continues to work with judges and experts involved in climate change litigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nancy Pelosi hospitalized after she 'sustained an injury' from fall on official trip to Luxembourg
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi has been hospitalized after she “sustained an injury” during an official engagement in Luxembourg, according to a spokesman.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pelosi hospitalized in Luxembourg after sustaining injury from fall on stairs
item.description: Former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) was hospitalized for evaluation in Luxembourg after falling on a set of stairs and sustaining an injury during a trip with a congressional delegation.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Montana Supreme Court blocks ban on transgender surgeries, prompting outcry from GOP lawmaker, supporters
item.description: A Montana Republican lawmaker is criticizing a state Supreme Court's decision to continue blocking a ban on transgender surgeries for minors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden stirs outrage in Scranton by commuting 'kids for cash' judge's sentence
item.description: President Biden has stirred controversy after he commuted the sentence of a corrupt judge who was jailed for more than 17 years after he was caught taking kickbacks for sending juveniles to for-profit detention facilities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey drone sightings: Lawmakers press for more briefings
item.description: "Law enforcement and other agencies continue to support New Jersey and investigate the reports," the FBI said.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: A nonprofit leader, a social worker: Here are the stories of the people on Biden's clemency list
item.description: Among the roughly 1,500 people whose sentences were commuted or who were pardoned by President Joe Biden are a nonprofit leader who supports at-risk youth, a social worker who fosters animals and a postdoctoral researcher One woman who received a pardon said she wanted people with drug addiction to
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gabbard struggles to woo senators ahead of confirmation fight
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard is struggling through her meetings with senators this week, sources told The Hill, highlighting the difficult path she faces in winning confirmation to be director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota cities take different approaches to recreational cannabis market
item.description: The Minnesota Legislature set most of the rules about cultivation and sale of recreational marijuana in spring 2023. However, the state left several key decisions within local control, including whether cities can allow more than a set minimum of shops.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Mitt Romney's Senate exit may create a vacuum of vocal, conservative Trump critics
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — With Mitt Romney set to exit the U.S. Senate, Washington will be without one of its strongest conservative critics of Donald Trump when the president retakes the White House in the new year.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watchdog finds FBI intelligence missteps before Jan. 6 riot, but no undercover agents were present
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI should have done more to gather intelligence before the Capitol riot, according to a watchdog report Thursday that also said no undercover FBI employees were on the scene on Jan. 6, 2021, and none of the bureau’s informants was authorized to participate.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: O’Malley: Calling Social Security a 'legal Ponzi scheme' is bull
item.description: "Social Security is not on a path for bankruptcy or insolvency," O'Malley said during a Thursday appearance on "The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Ontario to restrict electricity exports to US and bar American-made alcohol if Trump tariffs applied
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada’s most populous province may bar American-made alcohol in addition to restricting electricity exports to Michigan, New York and Minnesota if U.S. President-elect Donald Trump imposes sweeping tariffs on all Canadian products, a senior official said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump offers support for dockworkers union by saying ports shouldn't install more automated systems
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday voiced his support for the dockworkers union before their contract expires next month at Eastern and Gulf Coast ports, saying that any further “automation” of the ports would harm workers.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC to fund guaranteed income program: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City will provide funding for a guaranteed income program to support soon-to-be mothers experiencing housing insecurity.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Senate begins final push to expand Social Security benefits for millions of people
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate is pushing toward a vote on legislation that would provide full Social Security benefits to millions of people, setting up potential passage in the final days of the lame-duck Congress. Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer, D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI Director Wray to resign at end of Biden administration
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray on Wednesday announced he would resign from the bureau at the end of the Biden administration next month, with more than two years remaining on his term in office.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson and Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: DFL, Republican senators weigh in on state budget forecast
item.description: The state’s latest economic forecast, revealed Dec. 4, predicts less revenue than expected and higher-than-expected costs for some government programs. MPR News host Emily Bright spoke with state Sens.
item.dc:creator: Emily Bright and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Trump trolls Trudeau as 'governor' of Canada
item.description: President-elect Trump mocked Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, calling him the "governor" of the "Great State of Canada.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: As session nears, status of a Minnesota lawmaker facing criminal charges remains a flashpoint
item.description: Sen. Nicole Mitchell has upcoming court hearings in a burglary charge ahead of a trial that would start soon after 2025 Legislature gavels in. She’s resisting calls to step down, and a departure would leave the Senate knotted up.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Biden is rushing aid to Ukraine. Both sides are digging in. And everyone is bracing for Trump
item.description: The grinding war pitting Ukraine against its Russian invaders has escalated ahead of Donald Trump’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Congress races toward fast-approaching government shutdown deadline
item.description: Congress has just 10 days until government funding is set to run out, and lawmakers don’t have a deal to keep the lights on during the holidays. Members on both sides of the aisle expect the government will stay open past the Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Lara Trump stepping down from RNC amid Senate speculation
item.description: Lara Trump is stepping down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee (RNC) as she considers replacing Florida Sen. Marco Rubio (R), whom her father-in-law, President-elect Donald Trump, picked to be secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5
